aiResponseFarmer: Thank you for the tomato leaf photo. It looks like Late Blight (Phytophthora infestans). An agricultural expert is reviewing the image to give you a customized treatment recommendation.
aiResponseDashboard: Late Blight suspected. Water-soaked dark green-to-black lesions observed on tomato leaves with fuzzy white growth on underside.
expertRecommendation: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Apply copper-based fungicide to surrounding healthy plants. Improve air circulation and avoid overhead watering.
messageToFarmer:
Expert Diagnosis: Late Blight confirmed.

Recommendation:
1. Pull out and safely burn/bury infected tomato plants immediately to stop the spread.
2. Spray healthy nearby tomatoes with a copper fungicide immediately.
3. Water only at the base of the plant in the morning. Do not wet leaves.

aiResponseFarmer: Thanks for submitting the cassava leaf image. It displays mosaic patterns characteristic of Cassava Mosaic Disease. We are verifying with an expert agronomist.
aiResponseDashboard: Cassava Mosaic Disease (CMD). Yellow-green chlorotic mottling, leaf distortion, and stunted growth observed in cassava foliage.
expertRecommendation: Plant resistant or tolerant cassava varieties in the next cycle. Use stem cuttings from healthy plants only. Control whitefly populations using neem oil.
messageToFarmer:
Expert Diagnosis: Cassava Mosaic Virus confirmed.

Recommendation:
1. Whitefly pests spread this virus. Spray leaves with organic neem oil solution.
2. Next planting season, make sure to purchase certified disease-free cuttings of mosaic-resistant cassava varieties.
